summary: Jazzy‑fusion groove with soaring UJB and epic Eyes→China sequence
blurb: The show rides a tight, jazzy‑psychedelic flow from an explosive Uncle John's Band opener through a masterful second set. Highlights include the soaring Eyes of the World → China Doll segue, a thunderous Caution Jam, and Keith's Herbie Hancock‑style Rhodes texture.
review:
On September 18 1974 at Parc des Exposition in Dijon the Dead delivered one of their most fluid late‑’70 performances. The first set launches with an unmistakable Uncle John's Band that immediately sets a high bar, followed by crisp renditions of Jack Straw, Friend Of The Devil and Black Throated Wind before slipping into a radiant Scarlet Begonias. Keith’s Fender Rhodes adds a Herbie Hancock‑style fusion sheen that carries through the set. The second half is pure fireworks: a blazing Eyes Of The World that segues effortlessly into China Doll, then into He's Gone, Truckin' and a muscular drum break that births an unforgettable Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam before the band rides it straight into Ship Of Fools. Phil’s bass thunders throughout, especially on Peggy‑O and the jam sections, while Bill Kreutzmann drives the improvisations with relentless energy. The soundboard capture from Miller is crystal‑clear and perfectly balanced, making this the definitive source for a show that feels both adventurous and tightly knit.

For Deadheads seeking the full experience, this recording showcases the band at a creative peak—jazzy, tight, and endlessly rewarding on repeat listens.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the same standout moments (Uncle John's Band, Eyes→China sequence, Caution Jam) and offering no consistent criticism of playing. The consensus describes the performance as 'legendary', 'tight', and 'jazzy‑fusion', justifying a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, well‑balanced mix and strong instrument separation; consistently praised as the most complete and clear version of the full show.

key_highlights: Uncle John's Band opener sets a high‑energy tone.
Eyes Of The World → China Doll segue delivers soaring, jazzy improvisation.
Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam builds into an epic Ship Of Fools climax.
Keith Godchaux's Fender Rhodes adds Herbie Hancock‑style fusion texture.
song_highlights: Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam

band_performance.Jerry: Twisting themes, rapid arpeggios and soaring solos on Uncle John's Band, Jack Straw and Eyes Of The World; described as life‑changing and edge‑of‑your‑seat playing.
band_performance.Phil: Front‑and‑center bass that ‘drops bombs’ and bounces around; standout solo on Eyes Of The World and powerful presence throughout the set.
band_performance.Keith: Fender Rhodes provides a Herbie Hancock‑style jazz‑fusion texture; piano and Moog sparkle on He's Gone, Scarlet Begonias and other moments, adding depth to the soundscape.
band_performance.Billy: Driving, supportive drumming that fuels the long improvisational sections; highlighted drum solo and tight kit work throughout.

must_listen_sequences: Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam
